/projects/orbit-dashboard.title: Egor Stasevich | Orbit Dashboard
/projects/orbit-dashboard.description: Clarity at a glance, power under the hood — Designed by Egor Stasevich, a product designer specializing in Web3, SaaS, and digital product experiences.
h1: I’m Egor. I make products work better.
h2: From data dump to decision engine
p: Designed a modular dashboard that helps users focus on what matters—without drowning in data noise.
Users were overwhelmed by dense layouts and lacked the ability to personalize or prioritize key metrics.
+50% increase in time spent on dashboard
Orbit needed a dashboard that could serve different roles—without sacrificing clarity. Whether you were a sales lead or ops manager, finding relevant metrics had to be fast, visual, and flexible.
We built Orbit’s dashboard around draggable, collapsible cards that users could configure to fit their workflow. Each module had its own interaction logic, status states, and filtering options.

By turning complexity into clarity, we gave teams a dashboard that worked around them—not the other way around. The redesign helped Orbit shift from being a data provider to a decision-making platform.

/projects/bloomly-onboarding.title: Egor Stasevich | Bloomly Onboarding
/projects/bloomly-onboarding.description: Turning onboarding into a moment of delight — Designed by Egor Stasevich, a product designer specializing in Web3, SaaS, and digital product experiences.
h1: I’m Egor. I make products work better.
p: Redesigned Bloomly’s onboarding to create a friendly, low-friction first experience that increased completion and long-term engagement.
Users dropped off during onboarding due to overwhelming screens, unclear instructions, and no sense of progress.
Positive qualitative feedback on tone and clarity

The first few moments with an app set the tone. Bloomly’s original onboarding felt more like a barrier than a welcome. Our challenge: make it simple, warm, and motivating—without sacrificing personalization.
We broke down the onboarding into bite-sized steps, each focused on one clear action. Soft animations, calming colors, and friendly language reduced anxiety and increased flow completion.

We also introduced optional customization—letting users go deeper if they wanted, but never forcing them through irrelevant screens. Clear progress indicators made the experience feel fast and purposeful.
The new onboarding turned drop-off into sign-up. By designing for clarity, tone, and ease, we created an experience that users actually enjoyed—and that paid off in retention and loyalty.

/projects/novapay-mobile.title: Egor Stasevich | NovaPay Mobile
/projects/novapay-mobile.description: Seamless money management in the palm of your hand — Designed by Egor Stasevich, a product designer specializing in Web3, SaaS, and digital product experiences.
h1: I’m Egor. I make products work better.
p: Designed and refined the user experience for a mobile-first fintech app focused on fast, intuitive money transfers and budgeting.
Users were overwhelmed by complex flows and unclear feedback during money transfers—creating hesitation and increasing drop-off.
92% task success rate for new users

In an industry where speed and clarity are everything, the smallest UX decisions can have outsized effects. NovaPay needed a mobile experience that felt effortless and secure—without overloading the user.
I mapped out the existing flows and found friction in key areas: redundant screens, unclear fees, and limited visual feedback. These added unnecessary steps to what should’ve been a seamless task.
Post-launch metrics showed the redesign had an immediate impact on both usability and user perception. By prioritizing simplicity and confidence, NovaPay became more than a tool—it became a habit.

/projects/11111.title: Egor Stasevich | 11111
/projects/11111.description: This is the project tagline — Designed by Egor Stasevich, a product designer specializing in Web3, SaaS, and digital product experiences.
h1: I’m Egor. I make products work better.
p: Streamline CRM was an outdated sales platform with a cluttered UI and inconsistent workflows. I led a full redesign to modernize the experience and improve usability for everyday tasks.
Users struggled with confusing navigation, inconsistent layouts, and too many steps to complete simple actions—hurting adoption and productivity.
40% increase in active weekly users within 3 months
Reduction in average task time by 35% for key workflows
Consistent 4.7/5 user satisfaction score in post-launch feedback
Internal teams reported fewer support requests and improved onboarding for new hires

Early user interviews and session recordings made one thing clear: the product was getting in the way of the process. Sales reps spent more time navigating tabs and buried actions than engaging with leads. Critical information—like contact history or deal status—was scattered across screens, often hidden behind dropdowns or modals. There was no clear hierarchy, and task flows required too many steps for even the most basic actions. This created friction, slowed productivity, and led users to rely on external spreadsheets and workarounds.

I began by mapping the most frequent workflows and identifying friction points at each step. From there, we focused on removing barriers—eliminating unnecessary clicks, surfacing essential data, and streamlining how users moved through lead and pipeline views.

The Streamline CRM redesign wasn’t just about aesthetics—it was about empowering users to work smarter and faster. By identifying real pain points, simplifying complex workflows, and designing with intention, we transformed a dated system into a product that teams actually enjoyed using. The measurable improvements in adoption, efficiency, and satisfaction proved that great design can drive real business impact.

/experience.title: Experience | Egor Stasevich
/experience.description: Explore Egor Stasevich’s professional experience in UX/UI and product design. Over 6 years shaping digital products for Web3, SaaS, and tech startups.
h1: 6+ years exp. Focused on real impact.
p: Led design of advanced Web3 trading products for both traders and investors. Created and scaled design systems, ran UX research, and prototyped high-impact features. Worked closely with engineers and product leads to ship polished, high-fidelity experiences.

Taught UX/UI fundamentals, interface design, and motion principles to groups of 10–15 students. Developed course materials covering Figma, user flows, prototyping, and product thinking. Mentored students through real projects and portfolio preparation.

Redesigned a full Web3 trading platform (v2.0 → v3.0) improving usability and performance. Streamlined trading flows, built component-based UI system, and introduced new interaction patterns. Delivered responsive, accessible, and high-impact product experiences.

Designed cross-chain swap interfaces for Ethereum, Polygon, and Arbitrum. Conducted competitor analysis, improved UX for transaction flow, and enhanced consistency. Delivered production-ready UI kits and web experiences for token launch.

Designed trading dashboards and tools for algorithmic and DeFi users. Created unified visual language for multiple products and simplified trading workflows. Focused on data visualization, usability, and seamless onboarding experience.

Conducted UX/UI bootcamps for beginners and junior designers. Taught product logic, usability testing, and visual design principles. Helped students transition into freelance and startup design roles.

Developed design solutions for data-driven marketing and analytics platforms. Worked on user flows, prototypes, and interaction patterns that improved user engagement and retention. Collaborated with marketing and development teams to create a unified experience across platforms.

Created website designs, landing pages, and marketing visuals for digital campaigns. Improved user experience through analytics and rapid prototyping. Balanced aesthetics with conversion-oriented product goals.
